#volume_levels: 0 counts as a volume level, so when volume_levels=50 the max volume level is 49
#dimensions: natural width and height of the vumeter
width = left_margin + dial_width + spacing + dial_width + right_margin
#files: The set of images required to render this VU-Meter
#rotaries: rotaries is an associative array of rotary definitions
a rotary defines image rotation parameters
for analog vu meters this image is typically the needle image
Fields of a rotary are:
enclosure: { w:width, h:height}
axle: { x:x-coordinate, y:y-coordinate} # with the enclosure
image: { w:width, h:height}
pivot: { x:x-coordinate, y:y-coordinate}
enclosure defines a rectangle relative to which rotation occurs,
and must be the dimensions of the VU meter dial
axle defines the point relative to the enclosure around which rotation occurs
this point may lie within or external to the enclosure
image defines the dimensions of the image to be rotated
pivot defines the point relative to the image around which the image is rotated
this point maybe within the bounds of the image, or external to it
When rendered, the pivot is overlaid on to the enclosure such that the pivot is exactly on top of the axle
For analog vu meters:
enclosure defines the dimensions of the dial
axle defines where the needle rotates around in the dial
pivot defines where the image is affixed to the axle
#placement: placement is an associative array of image aliases to coordinates
each placement specifies where an image associated with an alias will be rendered
#backgrounds: associative arrays keyed on background name
contents of each associative array are of associative arrays of channels
channels with names other the 'left' and 'right' or common, i.e. rendered always
channels are arrays of placements - there is no volume level associated with these placements
#levels*: The contents of each key must be objects which are associative arrays with 3 keys
'single' => only render the placement matching the volume level
'aggregate' => render all placements upto the volume level
'aggregateoff' => render all placements beyond the volume level
'hold'  : peak volume level is held for a brief time and rendered in non-aggregated manner,
lower volume levels are rendered to match sampled values
'decay' : (default) volume levels follows peak volume level decaying at configured rate, if lower
'hold+decay' : peak volume level is held for a brief time and rendered in non-aggregated manner,
lower volume levels follows peak volume level decaying at configured rate
1) decay is for use with VU Meters simulating analog VU Meters with needles
2) hold is for use with digital VU Meters, where multiple volume levels can be rendered in a frame
3) if peak is not set then volume levels are rendered to match sampled values
'placements': an associative arrays of volume level to placements
Each object does not have to contain placement references for each volume level
However the combination of objects SHOULD contain placement references for each volume level
vumeters#: associative arrays keyed on VU Meter name
contents of each associative array are 2 keys
levels: which set of levels in layout to use for the VU Meter. REQUIRED
background: which background in layout to use, if abseent then no backgorund is rendered
